name: NSP Post-Matric Scholarship Scheme for SC Students
description: Financial assistance to Scheduled Caste students pursuing post-matriculation or post-secondary courses to enable them to complete their education.
eligibility: SC category students studying in Class 11, 12, Graduation, Post-Graduation, or professional courses with annual family income not exceeding ₹2,50,000, and having secured at least 50% marks in the previous exam.
benefits: Reimbursement of compulsory non-refundable fees, academic allowance up to ₹1,200 per month for hostellers, and ₹550 per month for day scholars.
required_documents: Income Certificate (signed by authorized revenue officer)
Mark sheets of last qualifying examination
Fee receipt of current academic year

name: NSP Post-Matric Scholarship Scheme for OBC Students
description: Scholarships for Other Backward Classes (OBC) students for post-matric or post-secondary courses in recognized institutions.
eligibility: OBC category students in recognized institutions with annual parent/guardian income not exceeding ₹1,00,000, and having secured 50% or above marks in the qualifying examination.
benefits: Maintenance allowance up to ₹750 per month for hostellers and ₹350 per month for day scholars, plus reimbursement of tuition fees up to ₹20,000 per annum.

name: Pragati Scholarship Scheme for Girl Students (Degree)
description: An AICTE initiative to support advancement of girls pursuing technical education (degree level).
eligibility: Maximum of two girl children per family, admitted to the first year of a technical degree program in an AICTE-approved institution. Family income must be under ₹8,00,000 per annum.
benefits: Scholarship of ₹50,000 per annum for tuition fees, computer purchase, stationery, books, and equipment. No other additional allowances.
required_documents: Admission letter issued by central counseling authority
Declaration by Parents showing maximum two girl children

name: Saksham Scholarship Scheme for Specially-Abled Students
description: AICTE scheme supporting specially-abled kids to pursue technical degree or diploma courses.
eligibility: Specially-abled student with a disability percentage of not less than 40%, studying in AICTE-approved institutions, with an annual family income of less than ₹8,00,000.
benefits: ₹50,000 per annum for tuition fees and incidentals, plus a specialized equipment support grant based on disability category.
required_documents: Disability Certificate issued by competent medical authority

name: Central Sector Scheme of Scholarship for College and University Students
description: Financial assistance to meritorious students from low-income families to meet their day-to-day expenses while pursuing higher studies.
eligibility: Students above the 80th percentile in the relevant stream in Class 12 board exams, enrolled in regular graduation/post-graduation courses. Parental income must not exceed ₹4,50,000 per annum.
benefits: ₹12,000 per annum for the first three years of graduation, and ₹20,000 per annum for postgraduate courses.

name: Begum Hazrat Mahal National Scholarship
description: Scholarship scheme for meritorious girl students belonging to national minorities.
eligibility: Girl students belonging to Minority Communities (Muslim, Christian, Sikh, Buddhist, Jain, Parsi) studying in Classes 9 to 12. Must have secured at least 50% marks in the previous exam and family income under ₹2,00,000.
benefits: ₹5,000 per annum for Classes 9 and 10, and ₹6,000 per annum for Classes 11 and 12 to support school fees and study materials.

name: Pradhan Mantri Kisan Samman Nidhi (PM-KISAN)
description: Direct income support of ₹6,000 per year to landholding farmer families across the country.
eligibility: All landholding farmer families with cultivable land in their names. Excluded: Institutional landholders, taxpayers, and retired/serving government employees.
benefits: Financial benefit of ₹6,000 per year, paid in three equal installments of ₹2,000 every four months, credited directly to Aadhaar-seeded bank accounts.

name: Pradhan Mantri Fasal Bima Yojana (PMFBY)
description: A comprehensive crop insurance scheme safeguarding farmers against crop losses from natural calamities, pests, and diseases.
eligibility: All farmers growing notified crops in notified areas, including sharecroppers and tenant farmers. Voluntary for all farmers.
benefits: Insurance cover against crop damage. Premium rates payable by farmers are capped at 2% for Kharif crops, 1.5% for Rabi crops, and 5% for annual commercial/horticultural crops, with the rest subsidized by government.
required_documents: Sowing Certificate issued by local revenue authority

name: PM Krishi Sinchayee Yojana (PMKSY) - Per Drop More Crop
description: Promotes micro-irrigation systems like drip and sprinkler irrigation to improve water-use efficiency at the farm level.
eligibility: All categories of farmers who own agricultural land. Members of cooperative societies and self-help groups are also eligible.
benefits: Subsidies up to 55% of the system cost for Small & Marginal farmers, and up to 45% for other farmers to install drip and sprinkler irrigation units.
required_documents: Quotation of irrigation system from approved vendor

name: Pradhan Mantri Kisan Maan-Dhan Yojana (PM-KMY)
description: A voluntary and contributory pension scheme for small and marginal farmers to provide social security in their old age.
eligibility: Small and marginal farmers owning up to 2 hectares of cultivable land, aged between 18 and 40 years. Must not be covered under other pension schemes.
benefits: A monthly assured pension of ₹3,000 after attaining the age of 60 years. In case of death, the spouse is entitled to 50% pension.
required_documents: Consent form for auto-debit of monthly premium contribution (ranges from ₹55 to ₹200 based on entry age)

name: Paramparagat Krishi Vikas Yojana (PKVY)
description: Promotes organic farming and certified organic cultivation practices to improve soil health and crop value.
eligibility: Farmers belonging to clusters of minimum 50 acres or 50 farmers. Preference to small and marginal farmers.
benefits: Financial assistance of ₹50,000 per hectare for a 3-year period, of which ₹31,000 is given directly via DBT for organic inputs (seeds, bio-fertilizers, bio-pesticides).

name: Soil Health Card Scheme
description: Provides customized soil reports and fertilizer recommendations to farmers to increase productivity through balanced nutrient management.
eligibility: All landholding farmers in India. Soil samples are collected and tested once every two years.
benefits: Free soil testing and distribution of Soil Health Cards detailing soil nutrient status (macro, micro, and physical parameters) along with crop-wise dosage recommendations.

name: Pradhan Mantri Mudra Yojana (PMMY)
description: Collateral-free business loans up to ₹10 Lakh to micro and small non-corporate, non-farm enterprises.
eligibility: Proprietorships, partnerships, and small companies engaged in manufacturing, trading, or services. Loan amounts are graded in three categories: Shishu (up to ₹50,000), Kishor (₹50,000 to ₹5 Lakh), and Tarun (₹5 Lakh to ₹10 Lakh).
benefits: Collateral-free loans, low processing fees, reasonable interest rates, and issuance of Mudra Cards for working capital withdrawals.
required_documents: Aadhaar Card / PAN Card / Voter ID
Quotation of machinery/assets to be purchased
Business registration license or Udyam Registration

name: Stand-Up India Scheme
description: Promotes entrepreneurship among women and SC/ST communities by providing bank loans for greenfield business ventures.
eligibility: SC/ST and/or women entrepreneurs above 18 years of age. Green-field projects only (first-time venture). Non-individuals must have 51% shareholding held by SC/ST or women.
benefits: Composite loans (inclusive of term loan and working capital) between ₹10 Lakh and ₹1 Crore covering up to 85% of project cost, at lowest applicable interest rates.
required_documents: Proof of business address and incorporation
Balance sheets of last three years (for existing firms) or projected balance sheets

name: Startup India Seed Fund Scheme (SISFS)
description: Provides financial assistance to early-stage startups for proof of concept, prototype development, product trials, and market entry.
eligibility: DPIIT-recognized startups incorporated not more than 2 years ago, with a viable business model, technology incorporation, and not having received more than ₹10 Lakh monetary support under other government schemes.
benefits: Grants up to ₹20 Lakh for validation of proof of concept/prototype development, and up to ₹50 Lakh via debt/convertible debentures for market entry and scaling.

name: Credit Guarantee Fund Trust for Micro and Small Enterprises (CGTMSE)
description: Collateral-free credit facility to new and existing micro and small enterprises to encourage entrepreneurship.
eligibility: New and existing Micro and Small Enterprises (MSEs) engaged in manufacturing or services. Retail trade is also eligible.
benefits: Provides credit guarantee to lenders for loans up to ₹5 Crore, making it possible for entrepreneurs to get loans without providing third-party guarantee or property collateral.
required_documents: PAN Card and Aadhaar Card of promoters

name: Prime Minister's Employment Generation Programme (PMEGP)
description: Credit-linked subsidy scheme for generating employment opportunities through establishment of new micro-enterprises.
eligibility: Any individual above 18 years of age. Must have passed at least 8th standard for project costs above ₹10 Lakh in manufacturing and ₹5 Lakh in services.
benefits: Bank loans for projects up to ₹50 Lakh (manufacturing) and ₹20 Lakh (business/service). Capital subsidy of 15% to 35% based on location (rural/urban) and social category.
required_documents: Population Certificate (from local body for rural areas)

name: PM Street Vendor's AtmaNirbhar Nidhi (PM SVANidhi)
description: Special micro-credit facility for street vendors to access affordable working capital loans to resume livelihoods after Covid.
eligibility: Street vendors and hawkers vending in urban, semi-urban, or rural areas on or before March 24, 2020. Must possess Vending Certificate/ID card.
benefits: First collateral-free working capital loan of up to ₹10,000, with a 7% interest subsidy on timely repayment. Eligible for ₹20,000 second loan and ₹50,000 third loan upon repayment.
required_documents: Certificate of Vending or Letter of Recommendation (LoR)

name: Ayushman Bharat Pradhan Mantri Jan Arogya Yojana (AB-PMJAY)
description: The world's largest government-funded health assurance scheme, offering free hospitalization cover of ₹5 Lakh per family per year.
eligibility: Families listed in the Socio-Economic Caste Census (SECC) 2011 database, including rural households with specific deprivation criteria and identified occupational categories of urban workers.
benefits: Cashless and paperless coverage of up to ₹5,00,000 per family per year for secondary and tertiary care hospitalization, covering diagnostics, medicines, and pre/post-hospitalization costs.

name: Pradhan Mantri Bhartiya Janaushadhi Pariyojana (PMBJP)
description: Campaign to make quality generic medicines available to everyone at highly affordable prices through specialized retail centers.
eligibility: Open to all Indian citizens. Anyone with a valid doctor's prescription can purchase medicines from Janaushadhi Kendras.
benefits: Access to over 1,800 high-quality generic medicines and surgical items at prices that are 50% to 90% lower than equivalent branded medicines in the market.
required_documents: No documents required for general OTC products

name: Pradhan Mantri Suraksha Bima Yojana (PMSBY)
description: An extremely affordable, one-year accidental death and disability insurance cover for Indian citizens.
eligibility: All individual bank/post office account holders aged between 18 and 70 years who give auto-debit consent for the premium.
benefits: Accidental insurance cover of ₹2,00,000 for accidental death or total irrecoverable loss of both eyes/limbs. Cover of ₹1,00,000 for partial permanent disability, for a premium of just ₹20 per year.

name: Pradhan Mantri Jeevan Jyoti Bima Yojana (PMJJBY)
description: A government-backed, low-cost life insurance scheme offering life cover for death due to any cause.
eligibility: All bank/post office savings account holders aged between 18 and 50 years. Auto-debit premium consent is mandatory.
benefits: Life insurance cover of ₹2,00,000 in case of death of the insured due to any cause, at a low premium of ₹436 per annum.

name: Central Government Health Scheme (CGHS)
description: Comprehensive medical care facility for Central Government employees, pensioners, and their dependent family members.
eligibility: All central government employees, pensioners, members of parliament, judges, and specified eligible dependents.
benefits: Outpatient treatment (OPD) at CGHS wellness centers, free medicines, specialist consultation at government and empanelled private hospitals, and indoor hospitalization expenses reimbursement.
required_documents: Proof of Employment / Pension Payment Order (PPO)

name: Pradhan Mantri Matru Vandana Yojana (PMMVY)
description: Maternity benefit program offering cash incentives to pregnant and lactating mothers for the first living child.
eligibility: All Pregnant Women and Lactating Mothers (PW&LM), excluding those in regular employment with Central/State Governments or PSUs.
benefits: Cash incentive of ₹5,000 in three installments upon meeting milestones: early registration of pregnancy, antenatal check-up, and registration of child birth with first cycle of immunization.
required_documents: Mother and Child Protection (MCP) Card copy
Aadhaar Card of beneficiary and husband

name: Pradhan Mantri Awas Yojana - Gramin (PMAY-G)
description: Aims to provide a pucca house with basic amenities to all houseless householders and those living in dilapidated houses in rural areas.
eligibility: Families living in kachha or dilapidated houses in rural areas. Identified using SECC-2011 data and verified by Gram Sabhas.
benefits: Financial assistance of ₹1,20,000 in plain areas and ₹1,30,000 in hilly/difficult areas for house construction, plus ₹12,000 for toilet construction and 95 days of unskilled labor wage under MGNREGA.
required_documents: Sworn affidavit of not owning pucca house

name: Pradhan Mantri Ujjwala Yojana (PMUY)
description: Provides clean cooking fuel (LPG connections) to women from below-poverty-line (BPL) households to replace unhealthy traditional fuels.
eligibility: Adult woman belonging to an SC/ST, PMAY, Antyodaya Anna Yojana (AAY) or most backward class household, with no existing LPG connection in the household.
benefits: Free LPG connection with first cylinder and stove fully funded by the government. Subsidized refills up to 12 cylinders per year.
required_documents: Aadhaar Card of applicant and all adult family members

name: Atal Pension Yojana (APY)
description: A pension scheme targeting workers in the unorganized sector to secure their income post retirement.
eligibility: Any Indian citizen aged between 18 and 40 years holding a savings bank account. Must not be a taxpayer or beneficiary of other social security schemes.
benefits: Guaranteed minimum pension of ₹1,000, ₹2,000, ₹3,000, ₹4,000 or ₹5,000 per month after age 60, based on monthly contribution amount and entry age.

name: Sukanya Samriddhi Yojana (SSY)
description: Small deposit savings scheme specifically for the girl child, offering high interest rates and tax exemptions.
eligibility: Parents or legal guardians can open an account in the name of a girl child below the age of 10 years. Maximum of two accounts per family.
benefits: Highly attractive interest rate (compounded annually), income tax deductions under Section 80C, and maturity amount fully tax-free at age 21 or marriage after age 18.
required_documents: Birth Certificate of the girl child
Aadhaar Card and PAN Card of parents/guardian

name: Pradhan Mantri Garib Kalyan Anna Yojana (PMGKAY)
description: Food security welfare scheme providing free food grains to eligible beneficiaries to eliminate hunger.
eligibility: Families covered under the National Food Security Act (NFSA) - Antyodaya Anna Yojana (AAY) and Priority Households (PHH).
benefits: 5 kg of free food grains (rice/wheat/coarse grains) per person per month in addition to the highly subsidized regular NFSA allocation.
required_documents: Aadhaar-seeded POS authentication at ration shop

name: PM-VISHWAKARMA
description: Provides end-to-end support to traditional artisans and craftspeople to scale up their skills and market access.
eligibility: An artisan or craftsperson working with hands and tools in one of the 18 family-based traditional trades (e.g. carpenter, blacksmith, potter, tailor) aged 18+.
benefits: Vishwakarama Certificate & ID card, skill upgradation training with ₹500 stipend/day, ₹15,000 tool kit incentive, and collateral-free enterprise development loans up to ₹3 Lakh at a concessional interest rate of 5%.
